operator_action_items: After M-002 lands: docs/runbooks/observe-chatgpt-unknowns.md can be run anytime (manual observation, no tool) to resolve memo §7 unknowns — unblocks real-site claims for downloads/model-selection/session-pinning.

RESOLVED 2026-06-12 (operator): no pi concurrency cap exists; pi workers effectively unconstrained. The 'max 3' was an unverified predecessor spawn-config claim; corrected in team.json, charter, MISSION-002.md, RESUME. M-001 handoff's 'cross-mission pi allocation needed' signal is moot as a numeric ceiling.
After M-004: run real-site runbooks; then operator pushes (team never pushes).
apparatus_lessons: SELF-IMPOSED FICTION #2 (operator-corrected 2026-06-12): the '<=30 real-site messages/run, T2<=12, T3<=15' budget was invented by the team lead when codifying D-002, not stated by the operator. Removed everywhere (D-002, README, charter, team.json, MISSION-006+RESUME2, live prompt-file af09077). Real rule: human-paced, no programmatic spamming; audit log = transparency, not rationing. Symptom: the running manager skipped a UC3 continuity message 'to save budget.' Meta-pattern (twice now: pi-cap, msg-cap): my caution invents tighter limits than the operator's real ones — state the qualitative rule, ask for any number.

TRIPLE-LAUNCH apparatus bug (recurred 3x in M-006): a single ops-runner dispatch produced 2-3 concurrent managers (adjacent timestamps), each absorbed harmlessly by a manager-side atomic singleton lock (tmp/M-006-manager.lock). Net zero double-spend, but wasteful. Recommended durable fix: per-mission singleton guard (flock keyed on mission pointer) in claude-orchestrator-watch.sh. Likely trigger: ops-runner retry-on-perceived-slow-launch and/or remote-control session duplication.

MANAGER HEADLESS-DEATH #2 root-caused 2026-06-12: pi-worker-watch.sh default wait window (1800s) > manager Bash cap (600s); foreground watch errors at timeout; manager copes by backgrounding; backgrounded manager ends turn believing in re-invocation; claude -p exits. Fixes: (a) charter now prescribes the exact '--wait-seconds 480 foreground loop' recipe; (b) ops-runner contracts now include bounded auto-recovery (if manager exits with handoff MISSING and children alive/state!=DONE, relaunch with resume pointer, max 2) so recovery no longer routes through the team lead.

OPERATOR EMPHASIS 2026-06-12: best-of-N is the default and must not be narrowed for resource caution (pi is unconstrained). Verification = panel of distinct dimensions over ONE authoritative evidence run + synthesis (N>=3 for critical missions). M-002 T9 upgraded mid-flight from single verifier to N=3 panel; M-003/M-004 contracts must carry explicit panels.

OPERATOR CORRECTION 2026-06-12: the 'max 3 concurrent pi workers' cap was fiction — an unverified claim seeded at team spawn (team.json/charter/original-RESUME), enforced by nothing. Inherited resource numbers are CLAIMS to verify with the operator, not facts (agent-rigor: verify every claim, including those in your own contract).
Headless claude -p managers DIE at turn end — charter now carries detached-session discipline (foreground watches only). REWORK-CAUSE: spec-gap, cost ~3 min.

pi worker self-reported ACTUAL minutes are hallucinated (~15x off); telemetry v2: workers stamp date -Iseconds START/END_TIMESTAMP lines; manager derives ACTUAL from run-dir metadata + mtimes (in M-002 contract onward).
Run-dir/status-file persistence made the manager-death recovery zero-loss; keep requiring resume-ready state files in every mission.
Detached tmux missions survive team-lead session crashes; on resume, re-anchor from git log + handoffs + tmux ls before acting.
notes: Ops-runner pattern: background Sonnet agent, watch calls --wait-seconds 480 (Bash 600s cap), shipment verification = files+STATUS+VERDICT, digest back. M-001 ran 2 managers (death+relaunch) — see orchestration/handoffs/MISSION-001-handoff.json telemetry.
